prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial begins with a nearly black frame. Only archive watermarks are visible: a small “Classic Commercial Archives” logo at the lower left and a larger “@ClassicCommercialArchives” watermark across the lower middle/right.

By the middle of the clip, the scene has changed to a dark, studio-like setting. A light-colored, two-door sedan is shown in side profile, facing right. The car is brightly outlined against a very dark background, with its wheels and body contours visible. Above and behind it is a glowing object resembling a suspended light fixture or stylized display element. A pale, low shape appears on the left side of the background, but its exact identity is unclear.

The final frame shows a graphic end card or magazine-style layout on a black background. Near the top is an image of the same light-colored car in profile against a warm orange/yellow backdrop, with partially legible red/orange text above it reading “CONSUMER GUIDE …” or similar. Below the image, large white text reads “FORD FAIRMONT FUTURA.” At the bottom right is a Ford oval logo inside a box, and smaller text near the bottom appears to read “FORD DIVISION,” though it is somewhat blurred. The archive watermarks remain visible over the image.
